{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Otozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Otozuka Kofun Door.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|12=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|12=draft}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|12=draft}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|12=draft|12=draft}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|12=draft|12=draft}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|12=draft|12=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|12=draft|12=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|12=draft|12=draft}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|12=draft|12=draft}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:乙塚古墳 up to rev 101698247
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 乙塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Otozuka Kofun Door.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[土岐市]]泉町久尻1332（字勝負）ほか |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 21|緯度秒 = 55.93 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 10|経度秒 = 51.35 |ISO = JP-21 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺27.4m×26.1m
高さ5.8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gifu |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 乙塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''乙塚古墳'''（おとづかこふん）は、[[岐阜県]][[土岐市]]泉町久尻にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。付近の[[段尻巻古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。 == 概要 == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[段尻巻古墳]]（左）・乙塚古墳（右奥）}}]] 岐阜県南東部、[[土岐川]]北岸の河岸段丘縁辺部（標高約150メートル）に築造された大型方墳である。地元では[[八坂入彦命]]（第10代[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の娘の弟姫の墓として伝承される。[[2002年]]度（[[平成]]14年度）以降に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で（平成14年測量調査以前は円形とされた）、南北27.4メートル・東西26.1メートル・残存高さ5.8メートルを測る。墳丘外表で段築・[[葺石]]は認められていない。また墳丘周囲で周溝は認められていない。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。石室全長19.2メートルを測り、美濃地方では最大級の規模の大型石室として注目される。石室内の副葬品のほとんどは失われており、鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片（鳥形のつまみが付いた装飾付須恵器含む）・[[土師器]]片がわずかに検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定され、段尻巻古墳とはほぼ同時期の築造とされる。東美濃地域では代表的な大型古墳であり、当時の刀支評（[[土岐郡]]）を治めた有力首長の墓と想定される。方墳の採用には畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関わりが示唆され、ヤマト王権による地方の領域再編・支配の実態を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）に国の史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。現在は史跡整備のうえで公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎月第2日曜日に公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[安土桃山時代]]-[[江戸時代]]、陶工によって工房等として再利用。 * 江戸時代、陶祖神・山神を祀る場として信仰（[[天保]]5年（[[1834年]]）に陶祖神石祠の建立）。 * [[明治]]初年、塚の南に所在した乙塚庵が廃寺（昭和3年の小川栄一報告）。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）、文献上初見{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[1928年]]（昭和3年）、小川栄一による報告{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[1938年]]（昭和13年）12月14日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）、測量調査。方墳と確認。 * 2002-[[2005年]]度（平成14-17年度）、保存目的の発掘調査：乙塚・段尻巻第1-4次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2007年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年|2014]]-[[2015年]]度（平成26-27年度）、史跡整備に伴う確認調査：乙塚・段尻巻第5-6次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市文化振興事業団、[[2017年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[2017年]]（平成29年）10月13日、史跡範囲の追加指定。 * [[2018年|2018]]-[[2021年]]度（平成30-[[令和]]3年度）、史跡整備に伴う確認調査：乙塚・段尻巻第7-9次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市文化振興事業団、[[2023年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|2023}}。 * [[2019年|2019]]-[[2023年]]（令和元-5年）、史跡整備。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html 国指定史跡「乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳」]（土岐市ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：19.2メートル * 玄室：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.9-2.7メートル、高さ2.7-3.0メートル * 羨道：長さ5.4メートル、幅2.3-2.6メートル、高さ2.4-2.7メートル * 前庭部：長さ8.1メートル、幅2.6-4.2メートル 石室の石材は、主に近辺で産出する[[花崗岩]]の割石で、間詰石・礫床にはチャートを使用する。玄室の平面形は胴張形。奥壁は巨石1枚の上に小石材を詰め、側壁は3段積みである。玄門部はまぐさ石・立柱石によって構築される。床面は玄室の一部で礫床が検出されており（現在は本来の床面よりも20センチメートル程度上面で復元）、羨道から前庭部では小礫を詰めた排水溝が認められる。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では4枚。 石室内の副葬品のほとんどは失われており、発掘調査では鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片がわずかに検出されている。特に須恵器のうちでは、鳥形のつまみ（鳥鈕蓋）が付いた装飾付須恵器が出土例の少ないものとして注目される。 Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 - 1938年（昭和13年）12月14日指定、2017年（平成29年）10月13日に史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1270|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館]]（土岐市泉町久尻） - 乙塚古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 整備計画（基本構想・基本計画）|url=|publisher=土岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 整備計画|2017}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/f271032c752d4842ec2a5c3877ba5158.pdf|publisher=土岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}}} - リンクは土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2023|chapter=|title=史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/da4c5f8fd60c22f48879920e8f51d2e5.pdf|publisher=土岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|2023}}}} - リンクは土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]|year=1989|chapter=乙塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|乙塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=乙塚古墳|title=岐阜県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]21|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490212|ref={{Harvid|乙塚古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=|title=土岐市史|volume=第3巻（近代社会・土岐市の文化財）下|publisher=土岐市|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=平成16・17・18年度 土岐市市内遺跡発掘調査報告書 -乙塚古墳・段尻巻古墳・熊野神社3号墳範囲確認調査 |publisher=土岐市教育委員会・財団法人土岐市埋蔵文化財センター|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=牛丸岳彦|title=乙塚古墳と段尻巻古墳について -岐阜県土岐市所在の二古墳の調査-|date=1996-10-28|journal=専修考古学|publisher=[[専修大学]]考古学会|volume=|number=6|pages=59-65|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == *[[段尻巻古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1270|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html 国指定史跡「乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳」] - 土岐市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:おとつかこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[乙塚古墳]] to [[Otozuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 乙塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Otozuka Kofun Door.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[土岐市]]泉町久尻1332（字勝負）ほか |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 21|緯度秒 = 55.93 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 10|経度秒 = 51.35 |ISO = JP-21 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺27.4m×26.1m
高さ5.8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gifu |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 乙塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''乙塚古墳'''（おとづかこふん）は、[[岐阜県]][[土岐市]]泉町久尻にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。付近の[[段尻巻古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。 == 概要 == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[段尻巻古墳]]（左）・乙塚古墳（右奥）}}]] 岐阜県南東部、[[土岐川]]北岸の河岸段丘縁辺部（標高約150メートル）に築造された大型方墳である。地元では[[八坂入彦命]]（第10代[[崇神天皇]]皇子）の娘の弟姫の墓として伝承される。[[2002年]]度（[[平成]]14年度）以降に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で（平成14年測量調査以前は円形とされた）、南北27.4メートル・東西26.1メートル・残存高さ5.8メートルを測る。墳丘外表で段築・[[葺石]]は認められていない。また墳丘周囲で周溝は認められていない。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する。石室全長19.2メートルを測り、美濃地方では最大級の規模の大型石室として注目される。石室内の副葬品のほとんどは失われており、鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片（鳥形のつまみが付いた装飾付須恵器含む）・[[土師器]]片がわずかに検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃の築造と推定され、段尻巻古墳とはほぼ同時期の築造とされる。東美濃地域では代表的な大型古墳であり、当時の刀支評（[[土岐郡]]）を治めた有力首長の墓と想定される。方墳の採用には畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関わりが示唆され、ヤマト王権による地方の領域再編・支配の実態を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）に国の史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。現在は史跡整備のうえで公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎月第2日曜日に公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[安土桃山時代]]-[[江戸時代]]、陶工によって工房等として再利用。 * 江戸時代、陶祖神・山神を祀る場として信仰（[[天保]]5年（[[1834年]]）に陶祖神石祠の建立）。 * [[明治]]初年、塚の南に所在した乙塚庵が廃寺（昭和3年の小川栄一報告）。 * [[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）、文献上初見{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[1928年]]（昭和3年）、小川栄一による報告{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[1938年]]（昭和13年）12月14日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（指定名称は「乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳」）。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）、測量調査。方墳と確認。 * 2002-[[2005年]]度（平成14-17年度）、保存目的の発掘調査：乙塚・段尻巻第1-4次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市埋蔵文化財センター、[[2007年]]に報告）。 * [[2014年|2014]]-[[2015年]]度（平成26-27年度）、史跡整備に伴う確認調査：乙塚・段尻巻第5-6次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市文化振興事業団、[[2017年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}。 * [[2017年]]（平成29年）10月13日、史跡範囲の追加指定。 * [[2018年|2018]]-[[2021年]]度（平成30-[[令和]]3年度）、史跡整備に伴う確認調査：乙塚・段尻巻第7-9次調査（土岐市教育委員会・土岐市文化振興事業団、[[2023年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|2023}}。 * [[2019年|2019]]-[[2023年]]（令和元-5年）、史跡整備。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html 国指定史跡「乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳」]（土岐市ホームページ）。。 * 石室全長：19.2メートル * 玄室：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.9-2.7メートル、高さ2.7-3.0メートル * 羨道：長さ5.4メートル、幅2.3-2.6メートル、高さ2.4-2.7メートル * 前庭部：長さ8.1メートル、幅2.6-4.2メートル 石室の石材は、主に近辺で産出する[[花崗岩]]の割石で、間詰石・礫床にはチャートを使用する。玄室の平面形は胴張形。奥壁は巨石1枚の上に小石材を詰め、側壁は3段積みである。玄門部はまぐさ石・立柱石によって構築される。床面は玄室の一部で礫床が検出されており（現在は本来の床面よりも20センチメートル程度上面で復元）、羨道から前庭部では小礫を詰めた排水溝が認められる。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では4枚。 石室内の副葬品のほとんどは失われており、発掘調査では鉄製品片・[[須恵器]]片・[[土師器]]片がわずかに検出されている。特に須恵器のうちでは、鳥形のつまみ（鳥鈕蓋）が付いた装飾付須恵器が出土例の少ないものとして注目される。 Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳 - 1938年（昭和13年）12月14日指定、2017年（平成29年）10月13日に史跡範囲の追加指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1270|乙塚古墳 附 段尻巻古墳}}。 == 関連施設 == * [[土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館]]（土岐市泉町久尻） - 乙塚古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/f271032c752d4842ec2a5c3877ba5158.pdf|publisher=土岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第5次・第6次発掘調査報告書|2017}}}} - リンクは土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2023|chapter=|title=史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|url=http://www.toki-bunka.or.jp/tokibunka/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/da4c5f8fd60c22f48879920e8f51d2e5.pdf|publisher=土岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史跡乙塚古墳附段尻巻古墳 第7～9次発掘調査報告書|2023}}}} - リンクは土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]|year=1989|chapter=乙塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|乙塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=乙塚古墳|title=岐阜県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]21|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490212|ref={{Harvid|乙塚古墳（平凡社）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=|title=土岐市史|volume=第3巻（近代社会・土岐市の文化財）下|publisher=土岐市|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=平成16・17・18年度 土岐市市内遺跡発掘調査報告書 -乙塚古墳・段尻巻古墳・熊野神社3号墳範囲確認調査 |publisher=土岐市教育委員会・財団法人土岐市埋蔵文化財センター|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=牛丸岳彦|title=乙塚古墳と段尻巻古墳について So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Otozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Otozuka Kofun Door.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|12=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|12=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|12=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|12=draft}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|12=draft}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|qq=draft}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|qq=draft}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{citation needed|Otozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}. Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City|qq=}}, Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th century|qq=}} |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Toki City|ja|土岐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, and {{ill|Haji Ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th century|qq=}}, at the end of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later {{ill|Toki District|ja|土岐郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki District|qq=}}). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}, providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Otozuka Kofun]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]
