But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Otozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Otozuka Kofun Door.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternative name = |part_of = |location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Izumi-cho, Kuzuri 1332 (Shōbu) and surrounding areas |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 55.93 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 10|coordinates = 51.35 |omitted = JP-21 |type = {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = 27.4 m × 26.1 m
Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:土岐市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県にある国指定の史跡]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:土岐市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:土岐市の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{Commons category|Otozuka Kofun}}

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Toki City]] [[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{Commons category|Otozuka Kofun}}

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{Commons category|Otozuka Kofun}}

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{Commons category|Otozuka Kofun}}

Height: 5.8 m |burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Accompanying Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred Person = |Imperial Tomb = |Historic Site = National Historic Site "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun" |Tangible Historic Site = |Notable Points = |map = Japan Gifu |type = Hōfun |map_dot_label = Otozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Otozuka Kofun''' (乙塚古墳) is a {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Toki, Gifu|Toki City]], Gifu Prefecture. Together with the nearby {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}}, it is designated as a National Historic Site (official designation: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"). == Overview == [[File:Otozuka-danjirimaki 1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} (left) and Otozuka Kofun (right, in the background)}}]] Situated in southeastern Gifu Prefecture, on a river terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Toki River|ja|土岐川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庄内川}}, at an altitude of about 150 meters, Otozuka Kofun is a large-map_size rectangular Kofun. Locally, it is traditionally associated with Otomehime, the daughter of {{ill|Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|八坂入彦命|1=Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|comment=I know this does not exist but want the link to be standard across the wiki|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka-Iribiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Excavations have been conducted since 2002 (Heisei 14). The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. The total length of the chamber is 19.2 meters, making it one of the largest stone chambers in the Mino region. Most of the grave goods have been lost, but fragments of iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have been discovered. Among these, a decorative Sue Ware vessel featuring a bird-shaped knob is particularly noteworthy due to its rarity. The construction of the mound is estimated to date to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]], at the end of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun. Otozuka Kofun represents a key example of a large Kofun in the Higashi-Mino region, likely serving as the tomb of a powerful local leader governing the area known as Tōki no Kōri (later [[:en:Toki District, Gifu|Toki District]]). Its rectangular design suggests ties to the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]], providing insight into regional governance and power structures during the period.[https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website). The site was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 (Shōwa 13). Although it is open to the public following conservation efforts, access to the stone chamber is restricted and permitted only on the second Sunday of each month. == burial == [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Otozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south-southeast. area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 19.2 meters * Burial chamber: Length 5.1 meters, width 1.9–2.7 meters, height 2.7–3.0 meters * Passageway: Length 5.4 meters, width 2.3–2.6 meters, height 2.4–2.7 meters * Forecourt: Length 8.1 meters, width 2.6–4.2 meters The stone materials are primarily quarried granite from nearby sources, with chert used for infill stones and gravel flooring. The burial chamber has a barrel-shaped plan, with a rear wall composed of a single large stone topped with smaller stones, and side walls constructed in three layers. The entrance features a lintel stone supported by standing pillars. The chamber floor, partially restored to about 20 cm above its original level, includes a drainage channel filled with small gravel leading from the passageway to the forecourt. The ceiling comprises three stones in the burial chamber and four in the passageway. Most grave goods have been lost, but the discovery of a decorative Sue Ware vessel with a bird-shaped knob highlights the site's cultural significance. Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the rear wall) Otozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) Otozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward the burial chamber) Otozuka Kofun from the side.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun - Designated December 14, 1938 (Shōwa 13), with additional areas designated on October 13, 2017.{{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|ja|土岐市美濃陶磁歴史館|1=Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Mino Ceramic History Museum|13=User:Immanuelle/Toki City Historical Museum of Mino Ceramics|qq=}} (Izumi-cho, Kuzuri, Toki City) - Holds artifacts from Otozuka Kofun. == References == {{References Help}} {{reflist}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Danjirimaki Kofun|ja|段尻巻古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjirimaki Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Otozuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1270|Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun}} * [https://www.city.toki.lg.jp/kanko/bunkazai/1004852/1004853/1006503/1003298.html National Historic Site: "Otozuka Kofun and Danjirimaki Kofun"] (Toki City Website) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乙塚古墳|version=101698247|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Otozuka Kofun]] [[ja:乙塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Toki]] [[Category:Kofun of Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{Commons category|Otozuka Kofun}}

The mound is rectangular in type (previously thought to be circular before the 2002 survey) and measures 27.4 meters (north-south) by 26.1 meters (east-west) with a remaining height of 5.8 meters. There is no evidence of stepped construction or [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] on the mound's surface, nor of a surrounding moat. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening south-southeast. It is thought to have been built around the same time as Danjirimaki Kofun.
